document.docAuthor: Advay, Mahfouz, Rix, Martyn & Tekşen, Mehtap
document.docTitle: Fritillaria kordestanica M. Advay 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Fritillaria kordestanica (Liliaceae), a new species from western Iran

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Fritillaria kordestanica (Liliaceae), a new species from western Iran

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Department of Biological Science, Tehran University, Tehran, Iran & mahfouzadvay @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 3542 - 532 X
Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, United Kingdom & martyn. rix @ aol. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0001 - 9748 - 6163
Department of Biology, Aksaray University, 68000, Aksaray, Turkey & mteksen 2431 @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0003 - 0191 - 4229; for correspondence

paragraph.heading:
Fritillaria kordestanica M.Advay
,
sp. nov.
( Figs 1–4 , Table 1 , 2 )

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type :— IRAN . Kordestan : Marivan to Paveh , Avroman Takht (spelled Hawraman too.), Kamalla Village , grows in very wet and stony soils which are often in running surface water at flowering time,
1650 m a.s.l.
,
28 April 2012
,
Advay 13724
( holotype : Iran Kordestan Herbarium; isotype: HKS )
.

Fritillaria kordestanica
is distinct from related species by its combination of shiny green leaves with short stems usually less than 15 cm high and narrowly campanulate, dark brownish purple, often striped flowers, with a large basal nectary.
Fritillaria kordestanica
is similar to
F. uva-vulpis , F. chlororhabdota , F. assyriaca
in its narrowly campanulate flowers and habit ( Table 1 ).

Bulb-bearing herbs, bulb globose to ovoid, 10–25 × 13–20 mm , with 2–3 bulblets; tunics pale brown. Stem 10–26.5 cm long, erect or slightly curved, smooth. Leaves (3–) 4 (–5), sessile, shiny green. Lowest leaves 6.3–9.3 × 0.3–1.5 cm , alternate, lanceolate to oblanceolate, apex acute. Median leaves 4.1–6.3 × 0.3–0.9 cm , alternate, linearlanceolate, apex acuminate. Bract 1, 2.8–5.0 × 0.1–0.3 cm , linear, acuminate. Flower 1. Perianth narrowly campanulate, outer tepal of bud with a clear stripe, outside purplish grey, with a clear green stripe, inside greenish yellow or purplish, purplish brown. Outer segments 20–26 × 6–7 mm , ovate-lanceolate, acute to obtuse, recurved, ciliate-tufted at apex; inner segments 19–25 × 5–7.5 mm , ovate-lanceolate, obtuse, ciliate-tufted at the apex. Nectaries 4–6.1 × 1.0– 1.4 mm , ovate to broadly lanceolate, yellowish, placed 1 mm above tepal base. Filaments 6–9 mm long, swollen, papillose, slightly papillose towards the base, whitish yellow; anthers 5.5–8.3 mm long, oblong, apiculate, yellow, dorsifixed. Style 5.0–8.8 × 2–2.6 mm , cylindrical, greenish yellow, papillose, slightly trilobed to unbranched at apex; stigma entire. Capsule 13.6–20.7 × 15.7–16.7 mm , obovoid, rounded apically, tapering basally. Seeds 4.5–6.7 × 3.6–5.7 mm , flat, triangular-ovate.

caption.paragraph:
TABLE 1. Diagnostic characters
Fritillaria kordestanica sp. nov.
,
F. uva-vulpis
,
F. chlororhabdota ,
and F. assyriaca .

tr.td: 0.3–1.5 cm wide and lanceolate to oblanceolate
1.5–2 cm and narrowly oblanceolate to elliptic- oblong, usually folded at the fruiting time
rounded and narrowed at the mouth
outside purplish grey, rarely with a green stripe, inside greenish yellow striped or purplish brown
purplish grey with yellow tips, yellowish inside
13.6–20.7 × 15.7–16.7 mm, obovoid and rounded at apex
23–35 × 10–15 mm, cylindrical to narrowly obovoid and truncate
25–35 mm length, cylindrical to narrowly obovoid and truncate

subSubSection.paragraph: Etymology:— Referring to Kordestan Province (Western Iran ), where the new species was discovered.

Distribution, habitat, and phenology: —Known from four populations in Hawraman and Marivan, Kordestan Province , western Iran ( Fig. 1 ). It is an Irano-Turanian phytogeographical element and grows in wet meadows with running surface water at flowering time, 1300–1750 m . Growing with
Eranthis hyemalis ( Linnaeus 1753: 557 ) Salisbury (1807: 304)
,
Iris reticulata Marschall von Bieberstein (1808: 34)
,
Ornithogalum narbonense Linnaeus (1756: 15)
,
Muscari neglectum Tenore (1841: 49)
, and
Gagea graminifolia Vvedensky (1932: 269)
(Figs 2,3). Flowering March– April, fruiting June–July.

Conservation status: —
Extent of occurrence (EOO) is less than 5.000 km 2 , and the area of occupancy (AOO) is less than 500 km 2 (criterion B1, B2); number of locations is less than five (a), and with an estimated continuing decline (a, b) of occurrence (i), of occupancy (ii), quality of habitat (iii), and number of subpopulations (iv). Its populations are threatened by grazing. The developing capsules are usually grazed before maturation. This new species is assessed to be considered as “endangered: EN B1ab (i, iv) B2ab (ii, iii, iv)” ( IUCN 2019 ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 1. Distribution of
Fritillaria kordestanica
(blue circles),
F. uva-vulpis
(yellow squares),
F. assyriaca
(black triangles) and
F. chlororhabdota
(red stars) in Iran.

TABLE 2. Comparison of the morphological characteristics
Fritillaria kordestanica sp. nov.
,
F. uva-vulpis
, F.

subSubSection.paragraph:
chlororhabdota , F. assyriaca ,
and
F. pinardii .

paragraph.emphasis: ...Continued on the next page ...Continued on the next page

tr.td: 1, narrowly campanulate, outer recurved at the mouth
1, narrowly campanulate, rounded and narrowed at the mouth
outside purplish grey, rarely with agreen stripe, glaucous, inside greenish yellow striped or purplish brown
purplish grey with yellow tips, glaucous, yellowish inside
purplish outside with yellowish green striped from median extending to apex, sometimes tinged or sparsely green-dotted towards margins, glaucous, inside paler and greenishyellow striped
dusky purplish grey, glaucous outside, yellowish inside, obtuse
outside purple and yellow at apex, inside yellowish orange or greenish yellow or greenish
14–28 × 4–7, ovate to lanceolate, acute to obtuse
12–20 × 6.2–9, ovate, elliptic to narrowly oblong, obtuse
15-28 × 4-8 cm, lanceolate to narrowly lanceolate, acute or obtuse
9–26 × 3–8.5, lanceolate to obovatelanceolate, acute or obtuse
9–26 × 3–8, narrowly oblanceolate to narrowly obovate, obtuse
4–6.1 × 1–1.4, ovate to broadly lanceolate, yellowish, 1 mm above tepal base,
4–5 × 1–1.3, lanceolate (ovate- lanceolate), brownish-yellow, 1 mm above tepal base
2.5–4 × 0.6–0.9, linear-lanceolate, green, 0.5–1 mm above tepal base
2–5 × 1–1.3, linear- lanceolate, green, c. 1 mm above tepal base
1.5–6 × 0.5–1.5, linear-lanceolate, green, 0.5–1 mm above tepal base
6–9, whitish-yellow, densely papillose at upper part, slightly papillose towards the base
7–8, yellow-brown, swollen, densely papillose at upper part, glabrous to sparsely papillose at base
6–11, yellow, enlarged to base, all papillose
6–16, usually yellow, sometimes greenish-purple, enlarged to base, all papillose
slightly 3-lobed to unbranched at apex, smooth

cm,

Fritillaria kordestanica
habitat. A. Flowering. B. Fruiting.

Fritillaria kordestanica .

subSubSection.paragraph:
Additional specimens examined:—
IRAN . Kordestan : Marivan to Paveh , Hawraman area , Seline village ,
1730 m a.s.l.
,
28 April 2012
, Advay 13725 (Iran Kordestan Herbarium); Kammalla village ,
1750 m a.s.l.
,
26 April 2013
, Advay 13724 (Iran Kordestan Herbarium); Bendol village ,
1700 m a.s.l.
,
26 March 2013
, Ghaderi 13820 (Iran Kordestan Herbarium); Marivan, Kall village ,
1320 m a.s.l.
,
20 March 2014
, Advay 13870 (Iran Kordestan Herbarium); Rekhallan village ,
1300 m a.s.l.
,
25 March 2017
, Advay 14210 (Iran Kordestan Herbarium)
.
